items.headline: City updates youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: San José presented status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including youth empowerment and intervention programs, plus updates on library facilities and customer experience improvements. These programs affect how the city serves young residents and families.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8 with no new hearing date set. Residents expecting to weigh in on this development will need to wait for a rescheduled notice.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda does not specify the project details. Residents should monitor future updates to learn what construction work this contract covers.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project advances with consultant extension
items.summary: The city approved a third amendment to extend its consultant agreement with DKS Associates for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, indicating continued progress on this transportation safety infrastructure work.

items.headline: Street pavement replacement project bids awarded
items.summary: The city awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project following a competitive bid process, representing investment in street infrastructure repairs.

items.headline: City Council Updates Active Transportation Plan with New Scoring Criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including revised evaluation criteria for biking and pedestrian infrastructure projects. This plan will determine which streets and areas get priority for sidewalk, bike lane, and pedestrian safety improvements.

items.headline: Council proposes changes to vacancy filling process
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled, reducing some requirement (specific details not provided in agenda). This could affect how future council seats are handled if a member leaves office.

items.headline: Senior housing development coming to East St John Street
items.summary: Trillium is building a 65-unit senior apartment complex at 678 E St John St for $8.8M. This addresses San José's growing need for age-friendly housing as the population ages.
